title: U Michigan - Nerve Conduction Velocity

materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"ctoch","text":"Reagents and Materials:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5iieq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5atbk","text":"Equipment: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bcjm7","text":"  Nicolet VikingQuest Portable System with Nerve Conduction Studies ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":68}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"due9f","text":"  VikingQuest software run on Windows 7 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":40}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9f360","text":"   Nicolet 12mm .4mm diameter disposable platinum EEG subdermal needles ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c035g","text":"   Niclot disposable ground ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ee5j2","text":"   Heating lamp ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4n0e6","text":"   Infrared thermometer ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7shcl","text":"   Heating pad ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"kvnj","text":"   Flexible tape measure ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bmq5o","text":"   8”x 8” Styrofoam ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1mnoh","text":"   Isoflurane, 5% induction and 1-2% maintenance.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Motor tests o Duration .02 ms o Range 25 mA o low frequency filter 1 Hz o High frequency filter 10 kHz o Sensitivity 1 mV o Time 2 ms/div Sensory test o Duration .02 ms o Range 25 mA o low frequency filter 1 Hz o High frequency filter 10 kHz o Sensitivity 50 μV o Time 2 ms/div

To confirm the presence of diabetic neuropathy, nerve conduction velocity (NCV) studies are performed. The animals will be anesthetized with isoflurane. Body temperature is monitored with a dermal temperature probe and maintained at 34º C with a warming lamp during NCV. Skin temperature is maintained at 32ºC during NCV using an infrared thermometer. Body temperature is maintained at 34ºC after NCV using a warming pad to ease animal stress from anesthetic. The nerve studies will last less than 15 min per rat or mouse. The electrodes are cleaned with 70% alcohol between animals to maintain pathogen-free status. Sciatic-tibial motor NCV is determined by stimulating distally at the sciatic notch and distally at the ankle via bipolar electrodes with supramaximal stimulation. The conduction velocity is calculated using two the points of stimulation along the nerve and measuring the resultant onset latency and distance. Sural Sensory NCV is determined by stimulating the sural nerve distally at the ankle via bipolar electrodes with supramaximal stimulation and recording at the fourth and fifth digit. The conduction velocity is calculated using the onset latency and distance. Tail motor latency is determined by stimulating distally along the tail at a recorded distance of 3 cm. The onset latency is used for the latency measurement. Tail sensory NCV is determined by stimulating proximally along the tail at a recorded distance of 3 cm. The conduction velocity is calculated using the onset latency and distance.
creator: Lili Liang
